Um, if you're not familiar with reinforcement learning, this is basically like OpenAI kind of got its start with reinforcement learning. the environments. They had like a lot of really cool ones and this kind of concept where you basically have some environment, you send in your action to that environment and then in response to that environment like object you get um basically the next observation from the environment you get the reward if the episode was over or not and then any extra infos if your environment you know throws them out. This kind of like structure is pretty much what you see in all reinforcement learning environments. It's what I have personally used every single time I'm doing reinforcement learning. It just works really good. It's a great framework for thinking about reinforcement learning and then it makes it really easy to like swap in different algorithms and like change things and tweak things and it kind of it keeps things organized and it just seems like this is what everyone uses and will likely seemingly always use for this kind of thing where you have like a reinforcement learning environment.
